text:
MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Margaret Yuen is a 63-year-old woman with known Stage IIIB left upper lobe lung adenocarcinoma (EGFR L858R-mutant), treated with definitive concurrent chemoradiation followed by osimertinib consolidation/maintenance. She has been on osimertinib for approximately 14 months. Recent CT chest/abdomen/pelvis demonstrated interval enlargement of the left upper lobe residual lesion, a new right lower lobe pulmonary nodule, and a new subcentimeter right adrenal nodule, raising concern for systemic progression. MRI brain obtained urgently to evaluate for intracranial involvement.

TECHNIQUE:
Multiplanar, multisequence MRI of the brain performed on a 3.0 Tesla magnet. Sequences obtained include: axial T1-weighted pre- and post-contrast (gadolinium-based contrast agent, 0.1 mmol/kg IV), axial and coronal T2-weighted FLAIR, axial diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) with apparent diffusion coefficient (ADC) mapping, axial gradient echo (GRE/susceptibility-weighted), and 3D volumetric post-contrast T1 (MP-RAGE). Gadolinium contrast administered without adverse reaction.

COMPARISON:
MRI brain with and without contrast obtained approximately 14 months prior (at initial CNS staging, prior to chemoradiation initiation).

FINDINGS:

Supratentorial Compartment:
There is a new focus of abnormal enhancement within the right frontal lobe, located in the posterior aspect of the right middle frontal gyrus, measuring approximately 1.4 cm in greatest axial dimension. The lesion demonstrates vivid ring-like peripheral enhancement on post-contrast T1 sequences with a central region of restricted diffusion on DWI consistent with central necrosis. Surrounding this focus is a broad halo of T2/FLAIR hyperintensity involving the adjacent white matter, extending over approximately 2.8 cm, compatible with vasogenic edema. There is very mild local mass effect on the adjacent sulci without midline shift. No cortical involvement is seen. The lesion does not exhibit susceptibility artifact on GRE sequences to suggest hemorrhage.

A second, smaller enhancing lesion is noted in the left cerebellar hemisphere (see infratentorial compartment below).

The previously documented 4 mm extra-axial focus along the right parietal convexity, with a broad dural base, is stable in size and morphology compared to the prior study. The imaging characteristics remain consistent with a small meningioma. There is no associated edema, restricted diffusion, or interval growth to suggest metastatic involvement of this lesion.

No additional supratentorial enhancing lesions are identified. No cortical-based lesions or leptomeningeal enhancement along the cerebral convexities, interhemispheric fissure, or basal cisterns.

Infratentorial Compartment:
There is a new focus of punctate-to-small nodular enhancement within the left cerebellar hemisphere, adjacent to the left dentate nucleus, measuring approximately 0.7 cm in greatest dimension. Enhancement is fairly homogeneous on post-contrast sequences. There is no significant surrounding edema and no restricted diffusion. No local mass effect on the fourth ventricle.

The brainstem appears unremarkable. No mass lesions or abnormal enhancement identified within the pons, midbrain, or medulla.

Ventricular System and CSF Spaces:
The ventricular system is normal in caliber and configuration. No hydrocephalus. No midline shift. Basal cisterns are patent and normally configured. No evidence of tonsillar herniation.

Leptomeninges:
No leptomeningeal nodularity, thickening, or enhancement identified along the convexities, the posterior fossa, or the cranial nerve roots. The pattern is not consistent with carcinomatous meningitis.

Calvarium and Scalp:
The calvarium appears intact. No suspicious osseous lesions or marrow signal abnormalities. No abnormality of the scalp or subgaleal soft tissues.

Vascular Structures:
Major intracranial arterial flow voids are preserved. No large territorial infarct. No evidence of venous sinus thrombosis.

Other:
No acute intracranial hemorrhage. No acute ischemic changes. No restricted diffusion outside of the central necrotic portion of the right frontal lesion described above.

IMPRESSION:

1. Two new intracranial enhancing lesions compared to prior MRI brain approximately 14 months ago, consistent with newly developed brain metastases in the setting of known EGFR-mutant lung adenocarcinoma with concurrent systemic progression:
   a. Right frontal lobe (posterior middle frontal gyrus): 1.4 cm ring-enhancing lesion with central necrosis and surrounding vasogenic edema (~2.8 cm). Mild local mass effect on adjacent sulci without midline shift.
   b. Left cerebellar hemisphere: 0.7 cm nodular enhancing focus, no significant surrounding edema.

2. The previously noted right parietal extra-axial lesion (4 mm) is stable and unchanged; imaging morphology continues to favor a small meningioma rather than metastatic disease.

3. No leptomeningeal enhancement. No hydrocephalus. No evidence of herniation.

4. No additional brain metastases identified on this examination.

Correlation with the concurrent systemic imaging findings is recommended. The pattern and number of new intracranial lesions may be suitable for further discussion at multidisciplinary tumor board, particularly regarding the feasibility of focal intracranial treatment approaches given the limited burden of intracranial disease.

Interpreting Radiologist: [Attending Neuroradiologist Signature]
Dictated and Electronically Signed
